title: Add scene-level collaboration inbox with decision tracking for co-authors and editors
description:
## Problem
The app currently supports solo drafting but lacks a structured way to collaborate with a co-author or developmental editor. Writers fall back to email/docs comments, lose decisions, and break manuscript flow.

## Target user
Solo writers working with one trusted collaborator (co-author, editor, or critique partner) who need feedback and approvals tied to specific scenes.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active manuscripts with at least one collaborator interaction
- Reduce median time from feedback request to accepted scene decision
- Improve retention in revision-stage users

## Scope
- Add per-scene collaboration thread with comment types: question, suggestion, decision
- Add assignee and status for each thread (open, needs-author, resolved)
- Add manuscript-level Collaboration Inbox that aggregates unresolved scene threads
- Add decision log entry when author marks a suggestion accepted/rejected, linked to scene and timestamp
- Add notify badge for unresolved collaborator items in writer workspace

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-cursor editing
- Public/social community feed
- Billing, team-seat management, or organization admin controls

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads
- Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history
- Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context
- Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp
- Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status

## Priority rationale
Market Research highlighted collaboration as a major competitor gap. Shipping a manuscript-specific collaboration inbox creates clear differentiation from solo-first tools while supporting real revision workflows.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship narrative revision passes for arc consistency, promise/payoff, and voice drift
description:
## Problem
After first draft, writers struggle to run high-signal revision checks inside the app. Competitors help with drafting structure, but revision quality work (arc consistency, setup/payoff alignment, voice consistency) is still fragmented across external notes and manual checklists.

## Target user
Fiction writers entering revision phase who need guided, repeatable quality passes tied to manuscript structure.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of completed revision passes per active manuscript
- Reduce unresolved continuity/setup-payoff issues by final draft stage
- Increase manuscript completion rate after first-draft milestone

## Scope
- Add Revision Passes panel with three pass templates: Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift
- Each pass generates a checklist of scene-linked review items and statuses (open, accepted, dismissed)
- Allow writers to create custom notes per review item and mark final disposition
- Add pass summary view with unresolved items by chapter/scene
- Save revision history snapshots so writers can resume passes later

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting prose
- Grammar/spellcheck replacement
- General-purpose AI critique outside defined pass templates

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items
- Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note
- Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context
- Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later
- Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report

## Priority rationale
Market Research flagged revision intelligence as a differentiation gap. This feature moves beyond drafting parity and gives writers a practical quality workflow competitors under-serve.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Create submission package builder for query letter, synopses, and beta-reader packet
description:
## Problem
Writers can draft manuscripts but still face major friction when preparing submission deliverables. They must manually assemble query letters, synopses, and beta-reader materials in separate tools, causing inconsistency and duplicated effort.

## Target user
Writers approaching submission-readiness who need a repeatable handoff workflow from manuscript to external deliverables.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of completed manuscripts that generate at least one submission package
- Reduce time from draft-complete to first external submission/share event
- Improve perceived end-to-end workflow satisfaction

## Scope
- Add `Submission Package` workspace at story level
- Generate editable templates for: query letter, one-page synopsis, long synopsis, and beta-reader briefing sheet
- Auto-link core manuscript metadata (title, genre, logline, comps, protagonist/conflict placeholders) into templates
- Include versioning for package drafts with timestamps and status labels (draft, ready, submitted)
- Export package as DOCX bundle and plain-text copy blocks

## Non-goals
- Agent/discovery marketplace integrations
- Automated email sending or CRM workflow
- Legal contract generation

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create a submission package from an existing story in under 5 minutes
- All package documents are editable and save independently
- Metadata changes can be refreshed into templates with explicit user confirmation
- Writer can export package artifacts without losing template structure
- Package status and version history persist per story

## Priority rationale
Market Research identified publishing handoff as a concrete gap where competitors are weaker. This feature expands the app from drafting tool to full manuscript workflow, improving adoption and retention near the finish line.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
